title: Reverse Repo Counterparties: List & Eligibility Requirements

source.name: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

summary: The New York Fed Trading Desk publishes a reference list of reverse repo counterparties organized into three categories: banks, government-sponsored enterprises, and money market funds. The Desk conducts reverse repo transactions to help keep the effective federal funds rate from falling below the FOMC's target range. A recent addition (October 2025) shows TCW Central Cash Fund added to the money market fund category.
key_points: The NY Fed Trading Desk conducts reverse repo transactions to implement monetary policy by keeping the effective federal funds rate within the FOMC target range
Reverse repo counterparties fall into three categories: banks (16 entities), government-sponsored enterprises (17 entities), and money market funds (extensive list)
Bank counterparties include major institutions such as JPMorgan Chase, Bank of America, Citibank, Goldman Sachs Bank USA, and Morgan Stanley Bank
GSE counterparties include all 11 Federal Home Loan Banks plus Freddie Mac and Fannie Mae
TCW Central Cash Fund was added as a counterparty on October 3, 2025
The list includes both primary dealers and additional eligible reverse repo participants
why_it_matters: The stated significance is overstated. This is an administrative reference page showing counterparty eligibility, not a policy announcement or operational update. While it defines the RRP counterparty universe, it contains no new information or analysis that would affect market assessments.
market_structure_context: This page documents the counterparty eligibility framework for the Federal Reserve's reverse repo program. Reverse repos are used by the NY Fed Trading Desk to absorb excess reserves from the banking system and help maintain the effective federal funds rate within the FOMC's target range. The counterparties include banks, GSEs, and money market funds—all of which can park cash with the Fed overnight via this facility.

While relevant to market structure, it contains no news, commentary, or policy change. The only temporal data point is the TCW Central Cash Fund addition on October 3, 2025.
